FUNDAMENTALS

Altseason Playbook

💡 There's no other thing that interests the most in the crypto space: Altseason.

The moment in which the impossible becomes possible, when a small amount of money can turn into a big one. Every single investor, from the oldest to the newest craves the holy grail of altseason.

But this is something truly particular, that happens in a very short time window and in which we can find a lot of potential traps and pitfalls.

In this content I will explain the necessary steps to reach an altseason and which indicators you need to look at.

~ Definition ~

We can define an altseason as a moment of a crypto bull cycle in which altcoins outperform Bitcoin in both USD and BTC value, giving astronomical returns.

This usually tends to happen once Bitcoin is arriving at its final stage of a bull run, peaking or about to peak.

That is an important information, because it gives you the idea that the bull run is near the end, as altseason is a function of new participants entering the market.

~ Money flow ~

During past Bitcoin cycles, the altseason has been preceded by a simple money flow:

  • BTC → ETH → Large caps → Mid/Small caps → almost everything due to frenzy.

We also noticed multiple overlap phases in this bigger cycle.

~ Evolution ~

Altseason tends to start with new technologies first. Why? They drive the interest of speculators as they have "promising technologies" and more important fresh charts. Fresh charts means that they don't have solid resistance or support levels, thus traders are more inclined to trade and see how the PA could develop.

  • Undervalued altcoins

Projects with solid fundamentals, working products and also great tokenomics.

  • Old projects, meme coins and overall garbage

This is something that happened many times during a full-blown altseason. As new and dumb money flows in, people try to buy everything independently of the fundamentals. Everything pumps → huge warning signal. Never forget about it.

Additional note:

Old projects might pump, but they do not pump hard. The interest/speculation toward them has already gone so they just “catch up” with the market. This doesn't mean they couldn't offer decent ROIs, but there are better solutions out there.

💡 Indicators
"Ok mate, but which indicators can I use to gauge a potential altseason?"

In the following steps, I'll show the ones I use, taking note that this is a market in perpetual evolution. Over time, these indicators might change their relevance as the market matures.

Additional note: How can we gauge strength/weakness of a metric ? We're gonna use classic support/resistance levels and closures above/below.

~ ETH/BTC strength ~

Ethereum is the main indicator to identify altcoin's market strength, as it is the leader of this sector. The majority of the projects are built on it and ERC-20 smart contracts are the most utilized.

When it starts to outperform Bitcoin in Satoshi value, that is a strong sign that the interest/money toward altcoins is rising fast.

~ TOTAL 2 ~

TOTAL 2 simply shows the total market capitalization of the top-125 altcoins, excluding BTC. This is another important parameter that helps you understand new money flowing into the altcoin market by itself. You can clearly see how TOTAL 2 has been useful to evaluate market strength and weakness.

It is currently ranging in that macro range:

  • Flip range high → bullish and altseason
  • Lose range low → altcoins armageddon

~ BTC DOMINANCE ~

  • BTC.D increases → risk decreasing →Alts interest down → flat/down.
  • BTC.D decreases → risk increasing → Alts interest high → pump.

Bitcoin dominance spent multiple years inside the range, trying an initial break above the range high where it has been rejected, and then it started to correct which ended up with the break of range low and the confirmation of a massive altseason.

~ USDT DOMINANCE RATIO ~

This indicator shows the USDT dominance, therefore the USDT inflow/outflow in the crypto market.

USDT D. up → people moving to stables → bearish signal.

USDT D. down → people chasing altcoins → bullish signal.

~ ALTSEASON INDEX ~

The Altcoin Season Index tracks whether market sentiment points towards Bitcoin or altcoins. If 75% of the Top 50 coins performed better than Bitcoin over the last season (90 days), then the index deems it to be altseason.

At the current moment it shows Bitcoin season ongoing, as BTC has outperformed many altcoins and BTC D. is rising.

Take note:

This is an additional indicator and it shouldn't be used alone.Use it as a further confirmation after having analyzed the previous indicators.

💡 Further considerations

~ Matching indicators ~

If you take a look at the above-mentioned indicators, you'll also see how well they match each other.

  • BTC D. down
  • TOTAL 2 up
  • ETH/BTC strength
  • ALTS/USDT D. ratio down

All of them together during a bullrun → altseason.

There's also the TOTAL 3 that you can take a look at, which considers the capitalization from altcoins excluding BTC + ETH but since ETH becomes key in gauging the strength of the sector, I take it as a secondary addition.

~ The bark of the dog ~

Historically, $DOGE, one of the oldest cryptocurrencies (2013) has been an additional indicator to sustain an altseason. When it moved parabolic, it also created the assumption for altcoins to pump.

Why? We can assume that people buy $DOGE for its unit value (it's cheap and could give the illusion of becoming rich by buying a few bucks)

Here are the historical pumps of $DOGE. You can clearly see the correlation with historical Altseasons and $DOGE pumps. It's not a code law.

If we compare the 2 charts, we can see:

  • $DOGE 2020 November pump - no altseason.
  • $DOGE early 2021 pump - altseason.

So not a striker indicator, just something to add to our research.

~ ETH movement analysis ~

As we seen, Ethereum plays a key role to gauge a 𝗽𝗼𝘁𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗶𝗮𝗹 𝗮𝗹𝘁𝘀𝗲𝗮𝘀𝗼𝗻.

In this chart, I analyzed the 2017/2021 parabolic moves made by Ethereum during a full-blown altseason:

  • 2017 𝒑𝒓𝒆-𝒓𝒖𝒏: 49 𝒅𝒂𝒚𝒔
  • 2017 top : 49 𝒅𝒂𝒚𝒔
  • 2021 𝒇𝒊𝒓𝒔𝒕 𝒕𝒐𝒑: 42 𝒅𝒂𝒚𝒔
  • 2022 𝒔𝒆𝒄𝒐𝒏𝒅 𝒕𝒐𝒑: 42 𝒅𝒂𝒚𝒔

While the 2017 pre-run whole window started earlier, the final impulse lasted 49 days.

We don't know what the future will unfold, but if the average final pump of ETH lasts approximately 40 days, this could become a great additional indicator to spot a potential top.

This should give us the timeframe in which we can turn on our “alarms” and start to consider selling our bags.

~ BTC | TOTAL 2 peak correlation ~

We have previously seen what an altseason is: a function of new participants entering the market.

That being said since newcomers aren't aware of the right timing to enter, this is reflected in the charts, and TOTAL 2 more precisely as it is the index to gauge the altcoin market.

In this chart, we can see the correlation between Bitcoin’s peak (distribution phase) and TOTAL 2, which tends to peak slightly after BTC.

This could be another additional indicator to spot an altcoin market top since normies are and will be always late buyers.

💡 Final Takeaways

~ Things can change ~

This is a new market that it's constantly evolving.

It is likely that over time, the maturation process will change how indicators gonna work.

Examples:

  • BTC D. could have less impact as new crypto can be endlessly created.
  • Projects could maybe run with their own legs if adopted/regulated on a global scale.

Never take anything for granted and evaluate the market step by step.

~ Your altcoin should outperform BTC ~

There is no point in being invested in a project that doesn't outperform Bitcoin. Outperforming doesn't mean rising in USD but also in BTC value. Otherwise, you should be better to stay with the king.

~ Never develop an emotional attachment ~

Your coins are just..coins. They're not humans, they don't feel emotions. Once the altseason comes, you have to become a killer and take profits without hesitating.

~ Altseason playbook summary ~

To make things schematic and make sure you understand the concept, let's make a quick recap.

In analyzing a potential altseason, look for:

  • Bullrun impact (BTC breaking past ATH)
  • Closures above/below support/resistance of the above-mentioned indicators
  • Use weekly/monthly timeframes
  • Fear & Greed Index (Greed drives speculation)
  • People calling for "the next big thing"

Important note:

Twitter is full of “gurus” that constantly call for altseason. This is perfectly intended to farm engagement and also positions you in a “hurry” situation, forcing to rush into altcoins at any time. From my experience, this guide will truly help you to filter the noise and understand risk-on/risk-off periods, thus increasing your altcoin exposure or not. Stay focused and more importantly maintain a calm mind. This is the only way to make gains.

~ To conclude ~

Remember that altseason could produce insane gains but also collapse very quickly. The average period between peak & collapse is usually 2 weeks/1 month. Always take profits during the mark-up phase. Don't become exit liquidity.
